Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES via CourtCall:
Robert C. Rodriguez of GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (619-230-7436), appearing for defendant and moving party Hennessy Industries LLC.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties George Mezori and Shirley Mezori.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant failed to propound a separate all facts interrogatory as to punitive damages. Notwithstanding this omission, Defendant failed to provide the Court with Plaintiffs' complete response to special interrogatory no. 1, which seeks all facts supporting liability, and Plaintiffs' response to its request for production of documents no. 18, addressing punitive damages. (Declaration of Robert C. Rodriguez, Ex. C at 4:19-7-28; Ex. D at 5:4-5; see Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433, 1439 [holding "the defendant must make an affirmative showing that the plaintiff will be unable to prove its case by any means"].) Defendant's excerpts of Plaintiffs' response to special interrogatory no. 1 cuts off the section titled "Persons with Knowledge." (Declaration of Robert C. Rodriguez, Ex. C at 7:27.) Defendant also failed to address the depositions by Defendant's corporate representatives in other cases, including Defendant's PMK Craig Mountz, AMMCO's "dust collector" patents dated December 10, 1974 and April 1, 1975 and the corresponding patent applications, and the Industrial Hygiene Report for Ammco Tools, Inc. conducted on October 2, 1986. (Id. at 5:20-7:14.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Lori Anastasiou, CSR #4345, lori1csr@yahoo.com (Tel: 714-345-6124) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES via CourtCall:
Gilliam F. Stewart of CMBG3 LAW LLC (949-467-9503), appearing for defendant and moving party TMS International Corporation.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties George and Shirley Mezori.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant TMS International Corporation's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to present affirmative evidence as to its bankruptcy reorganization defense. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to provide evidence that Plaintiff was given adequate notice of the bankruptcy discharge via service by publication. (see Flores v. Kmart Corp. (2012) 202 Cal. App. 4th 1316, 1331.) Even assuming notice was adequate, the bankruptcy discharge does not affect the liability of Defendant with respect to recovering from its insurers. (Forsyth v. Jones (1997) 57 Cal. App. 4th 776, 782 [holding "we hold what another California court said in dicta: Section 524 permits an action against a discharged debtor to fix the liability of the debtor's insurers"].)

Defendant's requests for judicial notice as to Exhibits S, T, H, I, J, and K are denied. (see Johnson & Johnson v. Superior Ct. (2011) 192 Cal. App. 4th 757, 768 [holding "While we may take judicial notice of the existence of judicial opinions, court documents, and verdicts reached, we cannot take judicial notice of the truth of hearsay statements in other decisions or court files"].) Defendant's objections to the declaration of George Mezori are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok.
